headline: Iran: 68+ days of complete internet blackout + Hormuz standoff.
summary: Iran's internet blackout entered its 68th consecutive day — the longest documented in any country's history — with the total economic cost estimated at $5.2 billion.

headline: US tariff legal collapse: IEEPA struck down, Section 122 replacement also invalidated.
summary: The Supreme Court (6–3, February ruling) struck down all IEEPA-based tariffs as unconstitutional.

headline: Ukraine: largest drone barrage in months, first net Russian territorial loss in ~2 years.
summary: Ukraine launched ~500 drones overnight May 16–17, striking Moscow-area oil refineries and killing four.

headline: WHO declares Ebola PHEIC — Bundibugyo strain, DRC + Uganda.
summary: On May 17 the WHO Director-General upgraded a cross-border Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak to a Public Health Emergency of International Concern: 8 lab-confirmed + 246 suspected cases in Ituri Province (including health-worker deaths), plus 2 confirmed cases in Kampala with no apparent epidemiologi

headline: Switzerland: June 14 referendum on population cap; Bilaterals III in Parliament.
summary: The SVP's "No to 10 Million Swiss" initiative — backed by a record CHF 15.5M in declared campaign spend and polling neck-and-neck — would cap Switzerland's resident population at 10 million by 2050, compelling suspension of EU free movement if needed.

headline: GLP-1 drugs reduce cardiovascular events across 99,599 patients, benefits strongest without T2D.
summary: A pooled analysis of landmark cardiovascular outcome trials (LEADER, EXSCEL, HARMONY OUTCOMES, REWIND, SELECT; published in *JACC*) confirms significant reductions in MACE for GLP-1 receptor agonists.

headline: WHO declares Ebola PHEIC — Bundibugyo strain.
summary: _(See Week in Headlines.)_ The critical gap: no approved BDBV-specific vaccines or therapeutics exist — the trials producing rVSV-ZEBOV, REGN-EB3, and mAb114 were all designed for Zaire Ebola (EBOV).

headline: Microglia actively remodel fear-memory engrams during extinction learning.
summary: Brain-resident immune cells are recruited to the soma and dendrites of fear-encoding neurons when mice undergo extinction training.

headline: IDOL enzyme deletion slashes amyloid and restores neuronal resilience in Alzheimer's model.
summary: Ablating the IDOL enzyme from neurons (but critically *not* from microglia) sharply reduced amyloid-β burden, elevated APOE-regulatory receptors, and improved lipid metabolism and inter-neuronal signaling.

headline: mitoDREADD-Gs reverses dementia memory deficits without clearing amyloid or tau.
summary: An Inserm/Bordeaux team used a chemogenetic tool to boost hippocampal mitochondrial activity via designer receptor in frontotemporal dementia and Alzheimer's mouse models.

headline: DESTINY-Breast11 phase III: neoadjuvant T-DXd + THP raises complete remission by ~11 pp in HER2+ early breast cancer.
summary: In 927 high-risk patients, trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) followed by paclitaxel/trastuzumab/pertuzumab achieved pathological complete response in 67.3% vs 56.3% for standard dose-dense AC-THP (p < 0.0001).

headline: SMILE satellite launches May 19 with Swiss-built X-ray cooler.
summary: The ESA–CAS Solar wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer lifted off carrying an X-ray telescope cooling system built by FHNW Windisch and ZHAW (8 years of development).

headline: Uber: "Solving the Identity Crisis for AI Agents."
summary: Substantive engineering post on extending Uber's Zero Trust Architecture for agentic workflows.

headline: Stripe: "Can AI Agents Build Real Stripe Integrations? We Built a Benchmark to Find Out."
summary: Stripe built an agentic coding benchmark in production-realistic environments — full codebases, browser use, documentation search.

headline: Cloudflare Agents Week: production dogfooding data.
summary: Cloudflare published internal usage data from running their own AI platform: 20 million requests through AI Gateway, 241 billion tokens processed on Workers AI, 3,683 internal users.

headline: Efficient Exploration at Scale: 10× data efficiency for RLHF.
summary: [arXiv:2603.17378](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17378) · Asghari, Van Roy et al.

headline: LMArena methodology shift — direct battle votes now count.
summary: Starting May 2026, votes collected from direct-chat sessions count toward Elo — a significant methodology change making pre-May and post-May scores non-comparable.

headline: TanStack/TeamPCP supply chain worm: first documented SLSA Level-3 provenance bypass.
summary: On May 11, threat actor group TeamPCP hijacked TanStack's OIDC-backed CI/CD pipeline mid-workflow — capturing a trusted package-publish token from an attacker-controlled cache — and injected malicious artifacts into 42 `@tanstack` npm packages in six minutes.

headline: CVE-2026-41091 & CVE-2026-45498 — Microsoft Defender zero-days under active exploitation (CISA KEV, 20 May).
summary: CVE-2026-41091 (CVSS 7.8): the Malware Protection Engine improperly resolves links before accessing files, enabling local SYSTEM-level privilege escalation.

headline: CVE-2026-42945 / nginx-poolslip — dual NGINX crisis.
summary: CVE-2026-42945 (CVSS 9.2): heap buffer overflow in `ngx_http_rewrite_module` affecting NGINX 0.6.27–1.30.0, exploited in the wild since May 16 after PoC publication May 13.

headline: CVE-2026-20223 — Cisco Secure Workload CVSS 10.0: unauthenticated cross-tenant API access.
summary: An unauthenticated remote attacker can send a crafted HTTP request to an internal REST API endpoint, instantly gaining Site Admin privileges with read-write access across all tenant environments.

headline: MuddyWater (MOIS-affiliated Iranian APT) plants Chaos ransomware as false flag in corporate espionage campaign.
summary: Initial access: Microsoft Teams screen-sharing sessions to socially engineer employees into granting remote control.
